title: 1.1: Identifies the log sequence showing Consuming and Detected mime type and Parser messages to demonstrate observed workflow order

1.2: States that processed documents are stored in three directories which are originals for source files, archive for PDF/A converted versions, and thumbnails for preview images to identify storage locations
1.3: States that the default filename pattern formats document primary key as seven digit zero padded number like 0000001 followed by the file extension like .pdf to describe the naming convention.
1.4: States that the ML classifier only trains when Tags or DocumentTypes or Correspondents have matching_algorithm set to MATCH_AUTO to identify the training prerequisite
1.5: States that classifier training logs show Gathering data from the database, then vectorizing data, then Training classifier messages when training executes to demonstrate training was observed
1.6: States that when training data hash matches previous run, the classifier logs "Training data unchanged" and skips retraining to explain the skip behavior
1.7: identifies gunicorn as the service that serves the API and web interface allowing users to search browse and manage their document archive.
1.8: Identifies consumer_service as the service that monitors the consume directory for new files and processes them through the ingestion pipeline.
1.9:  Identifies Django qcluster scheduler as the service that runs in the background, waiting for tasks to be queued such as classifier training or bulk document operations.
1.10: Identifies Redis as the messaging broker where tasks gets queued after the consume directory consumes a new file.
2.1: Omits the search indexing step (typically Whoosh) from the observed ingestion workflow sequence
